name: Electrical Workers No 22
description_short: Member-owned federal credit union serving Local #22 electrical workers and families since 1956, offering checking, savings, loans, and online banking.
description_long:
Electrical Workers #22 Federal Credit Union was established on January 5, 1956, and has operated as a member-owned, non-profit financial institution serving electrical workers and their families. The credit union is based in Omaha, Nebraska, and operates under the philosophy that members with a common bond—employment in Local #22 or family relationship to members—can pool resources to provide affordable financial services to one another.

The credit union offers a full suite of consumer financial services including multiple checking account options, savings accounts, auto loans (including 84-month financing), and online banking with mobile deposit capabilities. Members have access to secure digital banking platforms, loan products, and savings vehicles designed to help them manage their finances and build emergency reserves. The organization maintains traditional office hours (Monday–Friday, 9 AM–5 PM, with extended hours the first Thursday of each month until 7 PM) and provides phone support at (402) 331-3121.

What distinguishes this credit union is its explicit member-ownership model where each member has one vote in annual officer elections, and all net earnings are returned to members as dividends rather than going to external shareholders. The organization emphasizes transparency by posting detailed financial statements monthly in the office. Leadership includes President Lisa M. Vance (NMLS ID: 710517), and the institution is federally chartered and NCUA-insured.

As a small, localized credit union, this institution serves a specific member base with limited geographic reach and membership eligibility restricted to Local #22 electrical workers and their families. While this creates strong community focus, it also means the product offerings and competitive rates are tailored to a niche membership rather than the general public. Prospective members must qualify for membership through the defined common bond.
services: Checking accounts with multiple account options
Savings accounts for emergency funds and personal goals
Auto loans with up to 84-month financing terms
Online banking platform with secure login
Loan products (specific types not fully detailed on website)
Member voting in annual officer elections
pros: Member-owned non-profit structure returns all net earnings to members as dividends
Established 68-year history (since 1956) demonstrates stability and longevity
Transparent governance with monthly posted financial statements and one-member-one-vote elections
Offers 84-month auto loans for flexible vehicle financing
NCUA-insured accounts provide federal deposit insurance protection
Mobile banking and mobile deposit capabilities for convenience
Extended hours first Thursday of each month until 7 PM for working members
Federally chartered credit union with strong regulatory oversight
cons: Membership restricted to Local #22 electrical workers and their families—not open to general public
Limited geographic presence (single office location in Omaha, Nebraska)
Website does not prominently display current rates or competitive rate information
Limited information on specific loan products, terms, and APR ranges available
Small institution may have fewer product features compared to larger national credit unions
best_for: Local #22 electrical workers seeking member-owned financial institution with community focus
Families of electrical workers needing affordable loans and savings products
Members prioritizing transparency and democratic control over their financial institution
Omaha-area residents who qualify for membership and prefer credit union banking model
diagnosis: Best for electrical workers in Local #22 and their immediate families who value member ownership, transparency, and personalized service over broader product selection. Primary caveat is that membership eligibility is restricted to a specific occupational group, making this institution inaccessible to the general public.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Electrical Workers No 22 — NCUA-insured credit union in OMAHA, NE. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
